title: Fragment: The Receipt
description: Found on the road outside Maple Heights after defeating the Tempo of Rush Hour. A delivery receipt, crumpled in the way of something that was in a pocket through every stop on a route that never ended, with an address that doesn't exist on any map.

lines: The road outside Maple Heights still had the quality of a place someone had just driven through very fast. Not damage — nothing broken, no skid marks. Just a particular aliveness in the air, the kind that lingers after something has moved through it with purpose.

Against the curb, flattened under one wheel's worth of pressure then lifted back by the wind: a delivery receipt. Yellow carbon-copy paper, folded and unfolded enough times to be soft at the creases. A barcode at the top, half-faded. A timestamp at the bottom.
The delivery address was in a standard font, printed clearly, but the street name didn't exist. The city didn't exist. The zip code was all zeros. The signature line had been signed anyway.

steps.text: Picking it up from the curb, the paper was still slightly warm from being carried in a pocket at speed. The Tempo's voice arrived between one breath and the next, moving the way it always had — not waiting to be listened to, just present for a moment, already past.

lines.speaker: Tempo of Rush Hour
lines.text: Every delivery on time. Every route optimized. I could run the whole district in under forty minutes if the lights cooperated.

lines.speaker: Tempo of Rush Hour
lines.text: I never once stopped to read what I was carrying.

lines.speaker: Tempo of Rush Hour
lines.text: There was always another stop. Always a window closing. Always a reason why reading it was a problem for the next leg.

lines.speaker: Tempo of Rush Hour
lines.text: I ran every route in this district. And I have no idea what any of it was for.

lines.speaker: Tempo of Rush Hour
lines.text: I hope whatever it was got where it needed to go.

steps.text: The receipt had the texture of something that had been important once, in a category of importance that hadn't required anyone to think about it. The address that didn't exist was printed clearly, matter-of-factly, as if the printer had known all along and dispatched it anyway. The signature on the signature line was neat, unhurried — the signature of someone who had finally had a moment to stop.
The sensation of moving very fast toward something and not minding, for once, that you don't know what it is.

steps.name: Tempo's Last Receipt
steps.description: A receipt for something delivered to nowhere, on time. Grants +3 SPD. The bearer always arrives. Tooltip: "Delivered. Destination unknown. On time."
